title: NOC 55109 – Other Performers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 55109 is a diverse category that captures the unique talents of variety artists who do not fall into traditional acting or dancing roles. From street performers and magicians to fashion models and puppeteers, these professionals add the final layer of wonder to Canada’s entertainment scene. At GTR Immigration, we recognize that "Other Performers" often have the high-level international visibility required for specialized immigration streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level5Major Group55 – Other occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group551 – Other occupations in art, culture and sportMinor Group5510 – Other occupations in art, culture and sportNOC Code55109

duties: Perform variety acts to entertain audiences at public and private events.
Practice and rehearse acts to maintain high levels of skill and safety.
Set up and maintain props, costumes, and specialized equipment.
Pose for photographers or artists to advertise products or for artistic creation (Models).
Engage with audiences to encourage participation and entertainment.
employment_requirements: Demonstrated talent and ability in the specific field of performance are the primary requirements.
Previous experience and a portfolio/video of past performances are usually required.
No specific formal educational requirements, though specialized workshops or training are common.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Performance opportunities are expected to grow in line with the expansion of the tourism and live entertainment sectors in Canada.
labour_market_demand: Demand is primarily seasonal and event-based, centered around Canada’s famous festival circuit (e.g., Calgary Stampede, Montreal Just for Laughs). There is also a consistent niche for models and commercial performers in the advertising hubs of Toronto and Montreal.
Festival Culture: Canada is home to some of the world’s largest street and fringe festivals.
Multicultural Stages: Opportunities to perform for diverse audiences in multiple languages.
Artistic Hubs: Access to collaborative spaces for multidisciplinary performers.
pathways: Self-Employed Persons Program: The most common route for performers who can prove self-employment in cultural activities.
Provincial Nominee Program (PNP): Selective opportunities based on local cultural initiatives.
Express Entry: For those employed by large entertainment organizations.
